title: gymnocladus assamicus
intro: Gymnocladus assamicus, also known as the Himalayan wrightia, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Gymnocladus assamicus include its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which can help protect against cell damage and infection. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tea, infusions, and tinctures, and is often used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat a range of ailments.
